questions.title: Pipeline Segmentation
questions.text: Should the skill define weighted pipeline value only at total aggregate level, or should it also address how to segment pipeline by business dimensions (e.g., region, practice, deal type, product line)?

choices.text: Total aggregate only — no segmentation guidance
Total aggregate plus segmentation by a standard set of cloud-services dimensions (region, practice, deal type)
Total aggregate plus guidance on choosing segmentation dimensions without prescribing which ones

notes.title: TCV choice has multi-year deal implications for cloud services pipeline
notes.body: The user chose TCV as the deal value basis. In cloud technology services, multi-year contracts are common. A 3-year $300K TCV deal at Lead stage (10%) would contribute $30K to weighted pipeline, while the same deal measured by ACV ($100K × 10%) would contribute only $10K. The user should confirm whether they intend TCV to include the full multi-year commitment or whether they expect an annualization step for multi-year deals.

notes.title: Q5 cross-method variance contradicts Q2 single-model choice
notes.body: Q5 answer 'All of the below' includes cross-method variance between fixed-weighted and historical-weighted pipeline, but Q2 selected fixed stage-based probability as the sole model. If only one probability model is in scope, cross-method variance requires a second model to compare against. This needs resolution.

questions.title: TCV Multi-Year Deal Handling
questions.text: You selected TCV (Total Contract Value) as the deal value basis. In cloud technology services, multi-year contracts are common — a 3-year $300K deal would contribute $30K to weighted pipeline at Lead stage (10%) using TCV, versus $10K using ACV. Do you intend TCV to represent the full multi-year commitment, or should multi-year deals be annualized before weighting?

choices.text: Full multi-year TCV weighted at stage probability — no annualization
Annualize multi-year deals first: ACV = TCV ÷ contract_term_years, then weight the ACV at stage probability
Weight TCV at stage probability for single-year deals only; annualize multi-year deals to ACV before weighting

questions.title: Cross-Method Variance Scope
questions.text: You selected 'All of the below' for edge cases, which includes cross-method variance between fixed-weighted and historical-weighted pipeline values. However, Q2 selected fixed stage-based probability as the sole model. Should the skill still describe historical-weighted variance as an advanced comparison pattern for organizations that later adopt a secondary probability model, or should cross-method variance be excluded since only one probability method is in scope?

choices.text: Include cross-method variance as an advanced pattern with a note that it applies when a secondary probability model is introduced
Exclude cross-method variance entirely — the skill covers only the fixed probability model
